pi_items.source: setup_task.sh: 52 initial log lines repeated 40 times (52 * 40 = 2080)
Analysis of log content in setup_task.sh heredoc (lines 317-370): only status codes 200 and 304 present; 404 does not appear in generated access.log
Verified in setup_task.sh: LogParser.java (lines 179, 189) and LogAnalyzer.java (line 207)

setup_task.sh line 311 comment states 'REAL lines from the dataset'; log format matches NASA format (Common Log Format with Eastern timezone -0400); all timestamps are 01/Jul/1995; verified via https://ita.ee.lbl.gov/html/contrib/NASA-HTTP.html that NASA-HTTP July 1995 dataset exists with 205.2MB uncompressed real access logs

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real NASA-HTTP data from July 1995 stored as a representative subset (52 base lines repeated 40 times = 2,080 total log entries, exceeding the minimum of 1500). The data source is authentic, following Common Log Format. The expected status codes include 404, but the actual generated access.log file only contains status codes 200 and 304. Three required methods (parseLogLine, parseLogFile, analyze) are present as stubs in the Maven project.
